title: WhatsApp Setup & Messaging
lede: Put your business on the world's most popular messaging app. This guide walks you A-to-Z through connecting your WhatsApp Business number to iMash, managing it, chatting with customers from a unified inbox, and creating Meta-approved message templates.

steps.title: Open the WhatsApp workspace
steps.content:
Everything WhatsApp lives on one powerful screen — your inbox, your numbers, your templates, and even visual bot builders.

  1. In the left sidebar under MESSAGES, click WhatsApp (or click Messages in the top navigation, then the WHATSAPP tab).
  2. The page opens on the Messages tab — your WhatsApp inbox. Across the top you'll see eight tabs:
  • Messages — the conversation inbox where you read and reply to customers.
  • Numbers — your connected WhatsApp Business numbers (the counter shows how many are active).
  • Templates — pre-approved message templates for messaging customers outside the 24-hour window.
  • Flows — Meta-native in-chat forms (appointment pickers, surveys, sign-ups).
  • Catalog — browse your product catalog and send product messages.
  • Bots — a visual builder for automated WhatsApp conversation flows.
  • Lists and Campaigns — audience lists and bulk template sends (covered in the WhatsApp Campaigns guide).

Two buttons sit at the top-right of the tab bar on every tab: a Refresh icon that reloads your credentials, and the blue Connect WhatsApp button.

steps.title: Connect your WhatsApp Business account
steps.content:
One click launches Meta's official Embedded Signup — no developer account, no API keys, no copy-pasting tokens.

  1. Click the blue Connect WhatsApp button in the top-right of the tab bar.
  2. A Meta popup window opens. Log in with the Facebook account that manages (or will manage) your business.
  3. Follow Meta's wizard: pick or create a Business portfolio, pick or create a WhatsApp Business Account (WABA), then add the phone number you want to message from.
  4. Verify the number with the code Meta sends by SMS or voice call.
  5. When the wizard finishes, the popup closes by itself and iMash shows Connected 1 WhatsApp number(s). Your number appears on the Numbers tab, and iMash registers it for sending automatically.
⚠️ If nothing opens when you click Connect WhatsApp, your browser blocked the popup — allow popups for this site and click again. Also note: a phone number cannot be active on the regular WhatsApp / WhatsApp Business phone app and the API at the same time; use a number you're ready to dedicate to the platform.

images.alt: Numbers tab empty state inviting you to click Connect WhatsApp to launch Meta's Embedded Signup
images.caption: The Numbers tab before connecting — click Connect WhatsApp to launch Meta's Embedded Signup

steps.title: Manage your numbers on the Numbers tab
steps.content:
Once connected, the Numbers tab becomes your control room. Numbers are grouped by Business Account (WABA), and each group header has an Add phone number button so you can attach more numbers to the same account — a quick 3-step wizard asks for the phone number and display name, then the verification code, and you're done.

Each number card shows at a glance:

  • The phone number and its verified business name.
  • Ready to send (green) or Not registered (yellow) — registration is what lets the number send via the Cloud API.
  • Quality rating — a colored tag appears if Meta rates the number YELLOW or RED (green quality shows no tag). Low quality usually means too many users blocked or reported your messages.
  • Messaging limit tier — how many unique customers you can start conversations with per day (Meta raises this automatically as you message responsibly).
  • An info icon revealing the technical IDs (WABA ID, Phone Number ID, Business ID).

And four actions on the right of each card:

  • Register — appears only if the number isn't registered yet; click it to activate sending.
  • Manage (gear icon) — opens the full management drawer (next step).
  • Sync from Meta (refresh icon) — pulls the latest name, quality, and tier from Meta.
  • Disconnect (red icon) — removes the number after a confirmation. You can reconnect later, but conversations may be interrupted.
💡 If registration fails with a PIN mismatch, the number already has two-step verification enabled: type its EXISTING 6-digit PIN in the box that appears on the card and click Retry — or disable two-step verification in WhatsApp Manager and retry without a PIN.

steps.title: Open Manage — profile, verification, and calling superpowers
steps.content:
Click the Manage gear on any number to open a drawer with nine tabs — everything Meta lets a business configure, without ever leaving iMash:

  • Overview — quick status and one-click actions for the number.
  • Calling — flip on WhatsApp Business Calling so customers can voice-call your WhatsApp number and your AI agent answers, just like a phone line. Audio is telephony quality.
  • Outbound — place business-initiated WhatsApp voice calls. WhatsApp requires per-customer permission first: request it with an approved Marketing template that includes a call-permission button (or free-form if the customer messaged you in the last 24 hours). Once the customer taps Accept, permission stays valid for 7 days or up to 5 calls in 24 hours, and you can place calls handled by the AI agent you pick.
  • Profile — your public business profile: about text, description, address, email, websites, and business category (vertical).
  • Display name — request a new display name (Meta reviews it: Approved, Pending review, or Rejected).
  • Verification — track your business verification status with Meta (Verified, Pending, More info needed, and so on).
  • Quality — a history of quality-rating and messaging-tier changes so you can spot trends.
  • Opt-outs — a read-only list of customers who opted out of marketing messages; iMash filters them out of campaign sends automatically.
  • Migration — move the number to or from another provider if you ever need to.
💡 Turn on Calling and your WhatsApp number becomes a full AI phone line — customers tap the call button in WhatsApp and your Smart Agent picks up 24/7.

steps.title: Work your conversations in the Messages inbox
steps.content:
The Messages tab is your day-to-day workstation: conversations on the left, the selected thread on the right, and a filter strip to slice through the noise.

  • Channel — All / SMS / WhatsApp. On this page it defaults to WhatsApp; flip to All to see SMS threads alongside.
  • Direction — All / Outbound / Inbound.
  • Sort — Newest, Oldest, or Most unread.
  • Date range — a quick preset (like Last 7 Days) plus exact from/to date pickers.
  • More filters — refinements in a popover: Entity (Technicians, Contacts, Leads, Other), Delivery Status, From Number, plus Unread only and Has media switches. A badge on the button shows how many refinements are active.
  • Bulk — select multiple conversations for bulk actions.
  • Search messages… — the box in the header searches names, numbers, and message text.

Click any conversation to open the thread and type your reply at the bottom — replies go out on WhatsApp automatically. Incoming messages from customers appear here in real time, and if a Smart Agent is assigned to the number, it can answer them for you.

steps.title: Start a new conversation
steps.content:
1. Click the green New Message button in the top-right of the Messages tab.
  2. Under Send To, pick who you're messaging: Technician, Contact, Lead, or a raw Phone Number. Picking a CRM record links the conversation to it automatically.
  3. Choose the Destination Phone Number (the recipient's saved numbers appear here — or type one manually).
  4. Choose the From Number. Once a WhatsApp number is connected, a channel toggle in this composer lets you send via WhatsApp instead of SMS — on this page it defaults to WhatsApp.
  5. Type your text in Message Body. Use the paperclip to attach an image or file and the smiley for emoji.
  6. Click Send Message.
⚠️ WhatsApp's golden rule — the 24-hour window: you can send free-form messages only within 24 hours of the customer's last message to you. To reach out first (or after the window closes), you must send an approved template — the composer offers a template picker for exactly this. That's what the next step is about.

images.alt: The New Message composer with Send To recipient tabs, destination and from number pickers and message body
images.caption: The New Message composer — shown here before a WhatsApp number is connected; once connected, a channel toggle adds WhatsApp sending

images.alt: The New Message composer with Send To recipient tabs, destination and from number pickers and message body
images.caption: The New Message composer — shown here before a WhatsApp number is connected; once connected, a channel toggle adds WhatsApp sending

steps.title: Create and submit message templates
steps.content:
Templates are your key to messaging anyone, anytime — Meta pre-approves the wording, and in return you can message customers outside the 24-hour window and run bulk campaigns.

  1. Open the Templates tab. If you have more than one Business Account, pick one in the Account selector — templates belong to a WABA, not a single number.
  2. The dashboard shows stat tiles (Total, Approved, Pending, Rejected, Paused), a Search by name… box, Status and Category filters, a Sync from Meta button (pulls templates created elsewhere, e.g. in WhatsApp Manager), and the New template button.
  3. Click New template. In the editor set:
    • Name — lowercase with underscores (e.g. appointment_reminder). Names can't be changed later; duplicate instead.
    • Language — the template's language code.
    • Category — Marketing (promotions, offers), Utility (order updates, reminders — cheaper per message), or Authentication (one-time passcodes only).
  4. Build the content: a Header (text, image, video, or document), the required Body, an optional Footer, and Buttons (quick replies, URL, phone call, and more). Insert variables like {{1}} for personalized values filled at send time.
  5. Marketing templates unlock premium extras: Carousel (swipeable cards), Limited-Time Offer, and the Call Permission Request button used for outbound calling.
  6. Click submit — the template goes to Meta for review and shows as Pending. Approval usually takes from a few minutes up to 24–48 hours; the status pill flips to Approved (ready to send) or Rejected (edit and resubmit).

Each row also has action chips: Analytics (delivery and read performance), Preview, Edit, Duplicate, and delete.

💡 Pick the category honestly — Meta re-categorizes mislabeled templates (you'll see a suggested category on the row) and can reject repeat offenders. Utility templates are cheaper than Marketing, but a discount coupon is Marketing no matter what you call it.

images.alt: Templates tab prompting to connect a WhatsApp Business Account first with a Go to Numbers button
images.caption: The Templates tab before a connection — templates are scoped to a Business Account, so connect one first

images.alt: Templates tab prompting to connect a WhatsApp Business Account first with a Go to Numbers button

steps.title: Go further: Flows, Catalog, Bots, Lists & Campaigns
steps.content:
Once your number is live, the remaining tabs turn WhatsApp into a complete sales channel:

  • Flows — build Meta-native in-chat forms (multi-screen questionnaires, booking pickers, lead forms) with a visual designer, then review customer responses in the built-in inbox.
  • Catalog — connect your product catalog, browse products, and send rich product messages customers can order from; track inquiries and orders.
  • Bots — a drag-and-drop builder for automated conversation flows: menus, keyword triggers, and hand-offs. For free-text AI conversations, assign a Smart Agent to the number instead.
  • Lists — build audiences from your CRM for bulk sends.
  • Campaigns — blast an approved template to a whole list, with per-recipient delivery tracking and automatic opt-out filtering. Full walkthrough in the WhatsApp Campaigns guide below.

That's it — your business now answers, sells, and even takes voice calls on WhatsApp, with AI doing the heavy lifting.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Stuck on Meta's signup, template approvals, or calling setup? Email support@imash.io and our team will walk you through it.

metaDescription: Connect WhatsApp Business to iMash with Meta's Embedded Signup, manage numbers and calling, chat from a unified inbox, and create Meta-approved message templates.
